learning_outcomes.description: Examine and evaluate the fundamental concepts of the mining process value chain, including ore fragmentation and mineral liberation principles.
Analyse the influence of geotechnical and geometallurgical data on fragmentation outcomes and final recovery across the stages of rock blasting, crushing and grinding.
Evaluate the key components of blasting engineering design, including objective setting, blast domain characterisation, drilling system selection, and explosive selection criteria.
Assess the relationship between explosive properties, detonation performance, rock mass damage mechanisms, and resultant fragmentation outcomes.

Apply analytical and empirical models to predict and evaluate blasting outcomes, including rock mass damage, fragmentation distribution, muckpile characteristics, ground vibration, and air blast overpressure.
Evaluate downstream process modelling requirements and design alternatives to achieve specific targets, incorporating socio-environmental considerations, and continuous improvement strategies.
Design and implement mine-to-mill optimisation strategies, using collaborative approaches to address complex operational challenges.
Evaluate design implementation procedures, safety protocols, and regulatory requirements, ensuring compliance with industry standards and best practices.
Work professionally and equitably as a group to analyse blast design alternatives using technical concepts and recommend an optimal design solution.

assessments.assessment_title: Completion of online module Quizzes and Portfolio of PBL Activities 1 to 5
assessments.task_description: Evidence of completion of online module quizzes and submission of portfolio of on-campus PBL activities 1 to 5.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Note that online module quizzes should automatically populate Blackboard after completion. Portfolio of activities 1 to 5 must be submitted through a link provided in Blackboard as a single PDF document on the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Group Drilling and Blasting Design and Optimisation project
assessments.due_date:
Progress update presentations 8/10/2026 2:00 pm

Final written report 23/10/2026 5:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
Details of the group Drilling and Blasting Project will be provided in Week 3 of the course. The overall mark will consist of two components:

- A brief presentation (progress report) worth 10% of the total mark  

- A final written report worth 90% of the total mark 

Note that a group peer review will need to be submitted to evaluate the contribution of each team member. The course coordinator reserves the right to vary group marks for each group member in the event of varied contributions to the team effort.

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Progress presentations in PowerPoint or PDF format must be submitted via the link provided in Blackboard by 2:00pm on 8/10/2026, prior to the commencement of the presentations.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The examination will cover all material taught during the semester.

All necessary formulae will be provided.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Live online course introduction session and Q&A

Online session which will provide an overview of the course structure, including online modules and Problem-Based Learning (PBL) activities. The session will outline assessment requirements and overall expectations. It will also introduce current and emerging challenges in mining, with a focus on the importance of fragmentation through drill and blast within the mining value chain. The aim is to set the scene, followed by discussion and a Q&A session.

The Week 1 date, time, and meeting link are provided in Blackboard.


Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_title: Live online course introduction session and Q&A

The session will outline assessment requirements and overall expectations. It will also introduce current and emerging challenges in mining, with a focus on the importance of fragmentation through drill and blast within the mining value chain. The aim is to set the scene, followed by discussion and a Q&A session. The Week 1 date, time, and meeting link are provided in Blackboard.

learning_activities.topic:
The Mining Value Chain

Complete the online module and quiz before the scheduled problem based learning activity. This module provides a brief description of theory of constraints and how it relates to mine process optimisation. This module also provides practical examples of the mining value chain and the impact of drill and blast on downstream activities.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: The Mining Value Chain Complete the online module and quiz before the scheduled problem based learning activity. This module provides a brief description of theory of constraints and how it relates to mine process optimisation. This module also provides practical examples of the mining value chain and the impact of drill and blast on downstream activities.

On campus PBL activity associated with the impact of blasting on downstream activities.

learning_activities.topic:
Rock blasting fundamentals

Complete the online module and quiz before the PBL activity. This module covers an introduction to explosive rock interaction, breakage and explosive performance. It also concludes with a brief description of the different types of blasting activities implemented in the mining industry.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Rock blasting fundamentals Complete the online module and quiz before the PBL activity. This module covers an introduction to explosive rock interaction, breakage and explosive performance. It also concludes with a brief description of the different types of blasting activities implemented in the mining industry.

learning_activities.topic:
On campus PBL session 2

This PBL activity focuses on various blast types, the key characteristics of explosive charges, and methods for quantifying their performance.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

Complete the online module and quiz before the PBL activity. This module covers the type of drilling, explosives and initiation systems used in mining activities.

Underground production blasting

Complete the online module and associated quiz prior to the PBL activity. The module adopts a practical approach to the design and implementation of underground production blasts, with a primary focus on uphole and downhole ring designs across a range of underground mining methods.

learning_activities.topic:
Mine process optimisation strategies focusing on Mine to Mill

Complete the online module and associated quiz prior to the PBL activity. This module outlines strategies that have been successfully implemented to optimise mineral extraction in open-pit operations, with particular emphasis on Mine-to-Mill and ore control approaches. Completion of the introductory mineral processing module in Week 10 is recommended to ensure a clear understanding of the optimisation strategies presented.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L06, L07

learning_activities.topic:
Revision, discussions and Q&A

The final session provides a revision of key course concepts, integrating material from online modules and PBL activities. It aims to reinforce understanding of core principles. The session concludes with an open Q&A, offering students the opportunity to clarify concepts, discuss challenges, and consolidate their learning in preparation for the final exam.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
